title: Phase-Reference Control of Steady-State Entanglement in Open Quantum Systems
authors: Areeda Ayoub, Alfonso Castillo-Gonzalez, Eric R Bittner
abstract: We show that steady-state entanglement in open quantum systems is controlled by the phase reference of a phase-sensitive reservoir. Using a covariance-matrix approach for Gaussian-preserving dynamics, we demonstrate that purely local, phase-sensitive dissipation can generate entanglement when combined with coherent coupling. The steady state exhibits a finite entangled region with an optimal squeezing strength that maximizes both the magnitude and thermal robustness of entanglement. We find that coherent coupling does not enhance entanglement monotonically, but instead regulates the conversion of local squeezing into nonlocal correlations. Importantly, the coupling dependence is controlled by the phase reference of the squeezed reservoir: phase-locked (rotating-frame) and laboratory-frame implementations yield qualitatively distinct steady states and entanglement structure. These results establish phase-sensitive reservoir engineering as a controllable route to steady-state entanglement in continuous-variable systems. Steady-state entanglement in phase-sensitive open systems depends explicitly on the reservoir phase reference and is not invariant under changes of that reference.}
summary: This paper shows that the phase of a squeezed reservoir can be used to control steady-state entanglement in open quantum systems. It demonstrates that local dissipation can generate entanglement when paired with coherent coupling, and that the effectiveness of this process depends on the phase reference of the reservoir. This establishes phase-sensitive reservoir engineering as a viable route for creating entanglement in continuous-variable quantum technologies.
main_problem: Investigating how the phase reference of a phase-sensitive reservoir can be used to control and generate steady-state entanglement in open quantum systems.
main_result: The authors demonstrate that local, phase-sensitive dissipation can generate entanglement when combined with coherent coupling, and that the resulting entanglement structure depends explicitly on the reservoir's phase reference.
method: A covariance-matrix approach for Gaussian-preserving dynamics is used to analyze the steady state of the system.
model_or_system: Continuous-variable open quantum systems subject to phase-sensitive (squeezed) dissipation and coherent coupling.
key_observables: Steady-state entanglement (quantified via entanglement measures for Gaussian states) and thermal robustness of entanglement.
important_parameters: Squeezing strength, coherent coupling strength, and the phase reference of the reservoir (comparing phase-locked/rotating-frame vs. laboratory-frame implementations).
main_assumptions: The dynamics are assumed to be Gaussian-preserving, allowing for the use of the covariance-matrix formalism.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the concept of phase-reference control, applies a covariance-matrix approach to Gaussian dynamics, analyzes the role of squeezing and coupling, compares different phase-reference frames, and concludes with the implications for reservoir engineering.
why_interesting: It provides a new mechanism for reservoir engineering, showing that the phase of the environment is a controllable knob for generating nonlocal correlations in continuous-variable systems.
analysis_basis: abstract only; PDF text extraction failed or PDF unavailable
